five_questions.problem: The LLM fine-tuning conundrum: adapting models efficiently while understanding internal layer roles is hard, and heuristic layer selection leads to suboptimal performance.
five_questions.hard: Hidden-state geometry is high-dimensional and noisy; RDP may mis-identify critical breakpoints, especially under trajectory instability or non-monotonic layer importance.
five_questions.tried: Traditional heuristics/random layer selection (e.g., random 13-layer) and full 36-layer adaptation; authors propose RDP-driven layer selection as an alternative.
five_questions.outcomes: RDP LoRA achieves 81.67% MMLU-Math using 13 RDP-selected layers, outperforming random 13-layer (75.56%) and full 36-layer (79.32%) on Qwen3-8B-Base.
five_questions.next: Reproduce RDP LoRA on Qwen3-8B-Base/MMLU-Math with 13 RDP-selected layers and evaluate robustness across runs/datasets to validate breakpoint stability.

grounding_issues: RDP LoRA uses only 13 RDP-selected layers
RDP LoRA significantly outperforms full 36-layer adaptation
RDP LoRA significantly outperforms random 13-layer selection
RDP LoRA significantly outperforms the baseline Qwen3-8B-Base model
